chunk_summary: Guidelines for iron prophylaxis in breastfed and preterm infants, including dosage, timing, and monitoring strategies, with emphasis on clinical and programmatic alignment.
learning_objectives: Understand the recommended timing and dosage of iron prophylaxis for breastfed term infants.
Learn the adjusted iron prophylaxis approach for preterm or low birth weight (LBW) infants.
Identify key monitoring strategies for infants receiving iron supplementation.
Differentiate between programmatic and clinical iron supplementation schedules.
key_facts: For term breastfed infants, prophylactic elemental iron is recommended at 1 mg/kg/day starting at 4 months of age until complementary foods are established.
India’s programmatic supplementation typically begins at 6 months, requiring clinical clarification for earlier initiation in high-risk infants.
Preterm or LBW infants should receive 2–4 mg/kg/day of elemental iron starting at 2–4 weeks of age, once clinically stable and tolerating enteral feeds.
Iron prophylaxis for preterm infants should continue through at least 12 months corrected age, with adjustments for iron content in formula or fortifiers.
Monitoring should include adherence, growth, feeding transitions, and symptoms like poor weight gain or intercurrent infections.
reference_quote: For term infants who are exclusively or predominantly breastfed, many pediatric guidelines support prophylactic elemental iron about 1 mg/kg/day starting at around 4 months of age and continuing until iron-rich complementary foods are reliably established. (Source: Indian Academy of Pediatrics Standard Treatment Guidelines)
document_key_topics: Iron supplementation for preterm and LBW infants
Monitoring and adherence in iron supplementation
